title: Is a biomass boiler right for your home?
subtitle: Assessing the practicalities of wood pellet or log gasification heating before you commit
excerpt: A wood pellet boiler producing 15 kW of heat uses around 3.5 tonnes of pellets per year in a well-insulated three-bedroom house, at a fuel cost of roughly 1,200 to 1,600 pounds per year at 2024 pellet prices. Against mains gas at 6p/kWh, the saving is marginal. Biomass becomes compelling for off-grid properties where heating oil is the alternative or where there is a reliable supply of low-cost cordwood.
sourceNotes: gov.uk Boiler Upgrade Scheme (BUS) eligibility rules 2024. Ofgem for fuel cost comparisons. BSI PD CEN/TR 15628 for biomass fuel quality standards. HETAS installer competence scheme guidance.

glossaryTerms.definition: Compressed cylindrical logs of dried sawdust or wood residues, typically 6 to 8 mm diameter and 10 to 30 mm long. Moisture content is standardised at 8 to 10 percent. ENplus A1-grade pellets have a calorific value of around 4.8 kWh/kg.

A wood-burning boiler technology that burns wood in two stages: first producing wood gas from the logs, then burning the gas in a secondary chamber at high temperature. Results in very high combustion efficiency (90 to 95 percent) and very low particulate emissions.

The UK government capital grant scheme for low-carbon heating. In 2024, biomass boilers replacing non-gas heating in rural homes were eligible for a 5,000 pound grant through an MCS-certified installer.

The EU Ecodesign Directive requirements for solid-fuel boilers, which set minimum seasonal efficiency and maximum particulate and NOx emission limits. Products meeting Ecodesign are labelled accordingly. Required for all new boiler installations in England and Wales from 2022.

content.text: A biomass boiler burns
wood pellets
, wood chips, or logs in a fully controllable way, with automatic feed systems for pellet and chip boilers, and manual loading for
log gasification

models. Sustainably sourced wood is broadly carbon neutral over the harvesting cycle. Whether a biomass boiler makes financial sense depends mostly on what you are replacing. Oil, LPG, and direct electric heating all make the comparison look good. Against mains gas at current UK prices, the saving is marginal at best.
The
Boiler Upgrade Scheme

(BUS) offers a 5,000 pound capital grant for biomass boilers in homes not connected to the gas grid. The property must have an EPC rating of at least D, a flue suitable for solid fuel, and an MCS-certified installer. Heat pumps attract a larger grant (7,500 pounds) under the same scheme, so run the comparative economics before choosing between the two.
The four questions to answer first

Is there a reliable fuel supply? Pellet deliveries require a vehicle access road wide enough for a delivery lorry and a 2 to 3 tonne bulk store sited within 20 m of the delivery point. For log gasification, you need a covered log store of at least 10 m3 that can hold a year's supply of air-dried hardwood.

Is there a suitable flue? Biomass boilers require a lined flue of at least 150 mm internal diameter reaching at least 4.5 m above the boiler or 600 mm above the roof ridge, whichever is higher. If the existing chimney is unsuitable or absent, a twin-wall stainless flue adds 1,500 to 3,000 pounds to the installation cost.
Does the boiler meet
Ecodesign
requirements? All new installations in England and Wales must use a compliant boiler. Non-compliant models cannot legally be installed. Check the product specification sheet before ordering.

What are the economics? Calculate the annual fuel cost at your chosen fuel type and compare to the current heating system. Include the amortised capital cost over 15 years, as biomass boilers typically cost two to three times more to install than a gas condensing boiler.
Pellet vs log gasification: the key differences

Pellet boilers load fuel automatically from a bulk store via an auger. They can be set up with a thermostat programme like any gas boiler and require minimal daily interaction. Log gasification boilers are loaded manually once or twice per day and require consistently dry hardwood (moisture content below 20 percent) for efficient combustion. Pellet fuel costs around 340 to 400 pounds per tonne (2024 prices); air-dried hardwood logs cost 80 to 150 pounds per cubic metre depending on species and region, but require significantly more storage volume per kWh of energy.
What done well looks like

The pellet store is sized for at least 3 tonnes, covers the winter supply without mid-season deliveries, and is weatherproof with a screw-auger outlet. The flue is lined and terminates at the correct height. The boiler is HETAS-registered and an annual service is booked. Fuel costs are 30 to 40 percent lower than the heating oil system it replaced.
What done badly looks like

The pellet store was sized at only 1 tonne, requiring three deliveries per winter and a delivery surcharge each time. Wet pellets from a poorly sealed store caused repeated auger blockages and incomplete combustion. The boiler was installed in a region with no local pellet supplier and bulk delivery adds 100 pounds per tonne in haulage. The economics work out no better than mains gas once delivery surcharges and servicing are included.
Troubleshooter

attrs.symptom: Pellet auger blocking every few days
attrs.cause: Pellet quality is below ENplus A1 standard: too much fines, broken pellets, or moisture above 10 percent
attrs.fix: Request a pellet quality certificate from the supplier. Switch to a certified ENplus A1 source. Check the store roof seal for water ingress. Purge the auger tube of broken pellet fragments before refilling with new stock.

attrs.symptom: Boiler generates visible dark smoke in cold weather
attrs.cause: Combustion air is insufficient or the fuel moisture content is too high, causing incomplete combustion
attrs.fix: For log gasification: check log moisture content with a wood moisture meter. Logs above 20 percent moisture will not gasify cleanly. For pellet boilers: check the combustion air damper setting. Have the boiler serviced and the combustion air ratio adjusted to the manufacturer's specification.

attrs.symptom: Annual fuel consumption is 25 percent higher than estimated
attrs.cause: The boiler is oversized for the heat loss of the insulated building, causing short-cycling and reduced seasonal efficiency
attrs.fix: If the boiler frequently modulates to minimum output and still overshoots the thermostat, add a buffer tank of at least 100 litres to decouple the boiler burn cycle from the heating circuit demand. This allows the boiler to complete a full burn cycle and store the heat rather than cycling on and off.
